items.title: Tasmanian government may have to pay $200m for Liberty Bell Bay remediation
items.summary: The clean-up bill for the Liberty Bell Bay manganese smelter site may ultimately may be paid by the Tasmanian government, according to legal experts, following administrators' failure to secure a buyer for the embattled operation.

items.title: US targets bridges, Iran hits Bahrain power plant as war threatens to expand in scope
items.summary: Washington and Tehran have been testing the limits of escalation since their ceasefire agreement collapsed last week, raising the prospect of a return to all-out war.

items.title: Irish national admits role in 'nightmare' plan to help alleged criminal flee Australia
items.summary: After agreeing to help smuggle an alleged drug trafficker out of Australia, Stephen Roche found himself at the centre of a living "nightmare", stranded in the outback without funds or friends, court documents have revealed.

items.title: Mother feared for daughter's safety before regulator ordered childcare centre to close
items.summary: A Canberra mother had concerns for her daughter's safety at Edge Early Learning in Higgins long before the ACT regulator ordered the centre to close.

items.summary: Harp therapist Alison Ware provides musical enrichment for animals at Canberra's National Zoo and Aquarium, adapting what she plays to suit different species.

items.title: Young Australians with bowel cancer say doctors missing the warning signs
items.summary: Georgie Cauchi had blood in her stool, constant pain and was struggling to get out of bed, but still in her 20s doctors told her she was too young to have bowel cancer. When it was finally discovered several years later, the tumour had grown so large it was blocking her entire bowel.

items.summary: Every Sunday, a group of men and women get together for some American clogging, home-baked afternoon tea and community connection.

items.title: Winter weather brings new and unique shells to southern WA beaches
items.summary: WA's southern coastline is covered in seashells, much to the delight of collectors. However, the state has a bag limit on how many you can take home.

items.title: Attacking the media for asking legitimate question is straight out of Trump's playbook

items.summary: A vote excluding women from elder positions in the Presbyterian Church in NSW and the ACT is disappointing but not surprising, a long-standing parishioner says.

items.title: One Nation's Malcolm Roberts falsely claimed COVID-19 vaccines caused miscarriages

items.summary: Growing up, Ioane Sa'ula thought he'd probably play rugby league professionally, but an injury scuppered his plans. Now, he's the romantic lead of new Aussie medical drama, The F Ward.

items.summary: Nikola Golem lied to his "mentally unwell" mother about her husband's death for more than a year, and let her sleep beside the decomposing body, Brisbane's Supreme Court heard.

items.summary: Will Warbrick's Melbourne Storm career appears to be over, with the departing winger rupturing his Achilles in his side's 14-6 loss to the Sydney Roosters.

items.summary: The Mapping the World's Prices report, published by Deutsche Bank, compares cost-of-living data in 69 cities across six continents.

items.title: Lauren Reynolds embraces motherhood at home UCI BMX Racing World Championships in Brisbane
